source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Large Scale Infrastructure: Variables storage and naming

questions.q: For maintainable large-scale Ansible, the most important separation is...
options: Separating the variables (data) from the Ansible code
questions.why: Correctly separating variables from code is what keeps a large site maintainable.

questions.q: Ansible can auto-load variables from folders based on...
options: The node's inventory name and its group memberships
questions.why: group_vars and host_vars are loaded automatically by group membership and hostname.

questions.q: In the recommended layout, where do group-specific variables go?
questions.why: group_vars/<group>.yml holds variables for that group.

questions.q: Where do variables specific to a single host go?
If node hostname1 belongs to group1, which variable files load automatically?
questions.why: Both the matching host_vars and group_vars files load automatically for that node.

questions.q: The chapter calls a server's inventory file its...
options: Identity card -- holding every variable that differs from the defaults
questions.why: The inventory file becomes the server's identity card of non-default variables.

options: Prefix with the role name and prefer flat names (e.g. sshd_config_permitrootlogin)
questions.why: Prefix variables by role and prefer flat names over dictionaries for clarity when centralized.
